---
title: "NY HP Rates: Data Quality Checks"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

This notebook checks the quality of gas bill data in the NY HP rates master tables. It compares gas bills before
(upgrade 0) and after (upgrade 2) heat pump installation, broken down by gas tariff transition, to verify that the gas
calc fix (`ny_20260307_r1-8_gascalcfix` batch) is producing sensible results.

## Setup

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Parameters

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Load data

We load the master bills for both upgrade scenarios (annual rows only) and the gas tariff maps that tell us which gas
tariff each building is assigned to.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

We also load the monthly ResStock load curves (local) to compute annual gas consumption per building for each upgrade,
so we can show the median consumption decline alongside the bill change.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Build comparison table

We join the two upgrade scenarios on `bldg_id`, restrict to natgas-heated buildings with nonzero gas bills in u0, and
attach the gas tariff keys from both upgrade maps.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Gas bill change by tariff transition

Each row shows how a group of buildings — defined by their gas tariff in u0 and u2 — fares after the heat pump upgrade.
We expect gas bills to decrease for most transitions since gas consumption drops \~89% after switching to a heat pump.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Summary by utility

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Consumption vs. bill change scatterplot

Each point is a gas tariff transition group. The diagonal line shows where points would fall if bills were purely
volumetric (no fixed charge) and the volumetric rate didn't change between u0 and u2. The gap between the data and that
line is the effect of fixed charges absorbing most of the consumption decline.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Building-level consumption vs. bill change

Same axes as above, but each point is an individual building rather than a tariff-transition group, faceted by electric
utility and colored by gas tariff transition. Low opacity reveals where buildings cluster — the horizontal banding
within each panel shows how different gas tariff structures compress bill changes relative to consumption changes.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```
